title: Children’s Overregularization and its Implications for Cognition
abstract: Abstract In particular, I propose that humans have both a statistical mechanism which extracts, collects, and tabulates statistical contingencies in the environment, and a rule mechanism which suppresses variation between examples within a category, thereby treating all instances of a category as equal members. Of course, neither mechanism is entirely novel; indeed, much of the debate in cognitive psychology concerns such mechanisms. My goal here is to argue that both mechanisms are necessary. Section 8.2 presents detailed arguments for the existence of both mechanisms within a single narrow linguistic domain, inflection. Section 8.3 proposes that these same two types of mechanisms are used in many other domains of cognition.
